_comment: Tier boundary numbers for the supervised load orchestrator (docs/ORCHESTRATOR_DESIGN.md #2). Proposed starting defaults, not scientifically derived. This project's own dogfooding history (ROADMAP #14: 5 of 100 mock Account inserts failed on a known DUPLICATE_VALUE:Jigsaw cause -- a 5% failure rate) shaped the general split -- a low single-digit ceiling for 'known, low-volume' (tier 2) vs a double-digit ceiling for 'probably systemic' (tier 3) -- rather than pinning the 2%/10% numbers exactly to that one run's own 5% (which itself would land in the tier-2/tier-3 band, not at the tier-2 ceiling). Otherwise chosen by rounding conservative per the design's own governing principle. Git-tracked, human-tunable -- same convention as batch_size_heuristics.json/field_synonyms.json. A human reviews and commits changes deliberately; nothing here is hardcoded in Python.

prod._comment: Materially tighter than uat, per the design's own requirement -- a clean UAT pass at a looser tier doesn't imply the same object should get the loose PROD tier (PROD access is separately gated by the graduation ladder in reference/orchestrator_trust_ladder.json anyway).
